geographicSummary: District Three encompasses the University District around Ohio State University, the Short North Arts District, Victorian Village, and extends north toward Worthington and west toward the Upper Arlington border. Its income and poverty levels are close to the city average, and it has a large student population that shapes its demographic profile.

pointsOfInterest.name: Short North Arts District
pointsOfInterest.description: Nationally recognized arts and entertainment corridor along High Street with galleries, restaurants, and boutiques.

pointsOfInterest.name: Wexner Center for the Arts
pointsOfInterest.description: Ohio State University's multidisciplinary contemporary arts center with free gallery admission.

pointsOfInterest.name: Goodale Park
pointsOfInterest.description: Columbus's oldest park, with playground, tennis courts, basketball court, and pond in Victorian Village.

pointsOfInterest.name: Columbus Museum of Art — The Pizzuti
pointsOfInterest.description: Short North satellite gallery featuring contemporary art exhibitions and the Caribbean-inspired Bar Room.

pointsOfInterest.name: Newport Music Hall
pointsOfInterest.description: America's longest continuously running rock club, located near the Ohio State campus on High Street.

pointsOfInterest.name: Ohio State University Campus
pointsOfInterest.description: Main campus of the state's flagship public university, one of the largest in the nation by enrollment.

areaCommissions.name: University Area Commission
areaCommissions.meetingSchedule: Third Wednesday of each month, 6:30 PM
areaCommissions.meetingLocation: Northwood & High Building, 2231 N. High St.

data.data_note: Figures from original site launch. Block-group weighted aggregation to council districts.

snapshots.source: U.S. Census Bureau ACS 5-Year via Census API (build_district_profiles.py)

data.data_note: Median income, rent, and home value are population-weighted averages of block-group medians — approximations, not true district medians. See /data/ for methodology.
